description_long: This is a mid-rise apartment building with a rectangular footprint and a flat, overhanging roof. The facade is clad in light brown brickwork, featuring a regular grid of windows on all sides. The most prominent feature is a central, black metal fire escape zigzagging down the front, providing emergency egress for each floor. The windows are symmetrically arranged in vertical columns, and there are no visible balconies or decorative elements. The building's overall appearance is utilitarian, with a focus on function over ornamentation. The structure is typical of early-to-mid 20th-century urban residential architecture found in dense city environments.
description: A rectangular, brick-clad apartment building with a flat roof, grid-like windows, and a central black metal fire escape on the front facade. The design is symmetrical and utilitarian, with no balconies or decorative features.
description_view_0: The front view shows the building's central black metal fire escape running vertically, flanked by evenly spaced windows in a brick facade.
description_view_1: The left side view displays a plain brick wall with a grid of windows, lacking any fire escape or additional features.
description_view_2: The rear view reveals a similar grid of windows in the brick facade, with no fire escape or distinguishing elements.
description_view_3: The right side view mirrors the left, showing only the brick wall and window grid, with no fire escape or other features.
attribute_window_arrangement: The windows are arranged in a highly regular, symmetrical grid pattern, with even vertical and horizontal spacing across all facades.
